insight: For Belgium, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, although they did manage to gain momentum in one out of the two breaks. When Belgium was pressing before a break, they struggled to maintain their intensity afterwards, losing momentum at a rate of -0.200 per minute, which suggests that breaks can disrupt their aggressive playing style. In contrast, when they weren't pressing before a break, they actually tended to gain momentum afterwards, at a rate of +0.100 per minute, indicating that breaks can sometimes provide a welcome opportunity for them to regroup and refocus.
